title: Support sessions terminate cleanly; queue updates live (dbg139)
description:
After dbg138, stale chat and remote sessions could block new invites: Mike's new chat request reopened old messages on his side without re-prompting Kat; orphan Postgres rows left remote "active" after admin ended connection; Clear Request / Cancel pending only cleared flags or pending remote — not active chats or remote control. The customer support queue modal did not show new requests until closed and reopened (Mike's SSE was on the global tenant channel while support-request events only hit the customer's tenant).

This release: new chat or remote invite always ends prior sessions for that customer–admin pair and broadcasts chat-ended / remote-ended before sending a fresh invite. Admin Clear Request terminates all chat + remote for the customer and clears the queue flag. Cancel pending and End connection menu actions fully terminate sessions. Customer widget resets on chat-ended / remote-ended; invite handlers allow re-prompt after terminate; stale localStorage persist token cleared when server has no matching session. Queue/presence SSE events fan out to the global admin channel so Mike's open queue modal refreshes within ~400ms.

Who is affected: Horizon admins and customers using PipeShare customer support (chat + remote) on the OVH private server. SaaS when Mike applies the platform version in releases.html.
changeLog: Backend: terminateCustomerSessions ends all pending/active chat + remote; broadcasts chat-ended and remote-ended.
chat/start and remote/request always create fresh pending invites (no reuse of stale active rows).
admin/clear-request and cancel-pending terminate sessions; new POST /saas/support/admin/terminate and /chat/end.
SSE support-request, presence, session events also broadcast to global admin tenant channel.
Admin queue modal refreshes on SSE without reopening; menu actions wire full terminate.
Customer: chat-ended SSE, reset widget state, clear orphan persist token; dbg139 cache bust.
